questions.question_text: Scientists continue to 'unravel' the mysteries of seismic activity through careful research. What does 'unravel' mean?

options.text: To create and add new layers of complexity to a problem
To ignore existing theories and start research from the very beginning
To gradually discover and understand something that was previously unclear
To publish final conclusions and close a scientific investigation

questions.explanation_ar: unravel تعني يكشف ويفكّ غموض، أي الوصول تدريجياً إلى فهم شيء كان معقداً أو غامضاً من قبل.

questions.question_text: Networks of seismometers 'monitor' seismic waves in real-time. What does 'monitor' mean in this context?

options.text: To destroy and remove all evidence of seismic activity
To continuously watch and track something over a period of time
To predict future events based only on historical data
To repair damage caused by seismic waves after an event

questions.explanation_ar: monitor تعني يراقب ويتابع باستمرار، أي رصد شيء ما بصفة منتظمة للكشف عن أي تغييرات أو تطورات مهمة.

questions.question_text: Modern earthquake detection has become 'sophisticated', allowing real-time monitoring of seismic waves. What does 'sophisticated' mean?

options.text: Simple, basic, and easy for anyone to understand and operate
Old-fashioned and based on methods developed many centuries ago
Highly developed, complex, and technically advanced in its function
Unreliable and producing results that scientists cannot fully trust

questions.explanation_ar: sophisticated تعني متطور ومعقد تقنياً، أي أن النظام وصل إلى مستوى عالٍ من الدقة والكفاءة في أدائه الوظيفي.

questions.question_text: When an earthquake 'strikes', it sends different types of waves through Earth's layers. What does 'strikes' mean?

options.text: Gives a clear advance warning before the main shaking begins
Occurs suddenly and hits with force without much prior warning
Weakens slowly and loses energy before reaching the surface
Retreats underground and avoids heavily populated areas

questions.explanation_ar: strikes تعني يضرب بقوة ومفاجأة، أي وقوع الحدث بشكل مفاجئ وعنيف دون تحذير يُذكر.

questions.question_text: Ancient earthquake science continues to 'baffle' researchers who try to fully understand it. What does 'baffle' mean?

options.text: To inspire and motivate scientists to continue their research
To confuse and puzzle someone beyond their ability to understand
To clearly explain and simplify a complicated topic for learners
To support and provide useful data for ongoing scientific research

questions.explanation_ar: baffle تعني يُحيّر ويُربك، أي أن المشكلة أو الظاهرة معقدة لدرجة تجعل العلماء عاجزين عن إيجاد تفسير مقنع لها.

questions.question_text: When engineers design a 'colossal' structure, they are building something unusually large and impressive in scale.
questions.explanation_ar: صحيح. colossal تعني ضخم وهائل الحجم، وتُستخدم لوصف الهياكل أو الأشياء التي تتجاوز الحجم المعتاد بشكل لافت.

questions.question_text: 'Meticulous' earthquake engineers make rough estimates and skip checking small details in their work.
questions.explanation_ar: خطأ. meticulous تعني دقيق ومنتبه لأدق التفاصيل، وهو عكس تماماً إجراء تقديرات تقريبية وتجاهل التفاصيل الصغيرة.

questions.question_text: Researchers use GPS technology to _____ the exact position of tectonic plates and track their slow movement.
questions.explanation_ar: detect تعني يكتشف ويرصد، وفي هذا السياق تعني استخدام التكنولوجيا لتحديد موقع شيء ما ورصد حركته.

questions.question_text: Which sentence uses 'could' or 'might' correctly in a second conditional to express a less certain result?

options.text: If we had better prediction tools, we might save thousands of lives.
If we had better prediction tools, we could save thousands of lives certainly.
If we have better prediction tools, we might save thousands of lives.
If we had better prediction tools, we can save thousands of lives.

questions.explanation_ar: يمكن في الشرط الثاني استبدال would بـ could أو might للتعبير عن نتيجة محتملة وليست مؤكدة. الخيار a صحيح، والأخرى تحتوي على أخطاء في الصيغة.

questions.question_text: Which sentence correctly uses the second conditional in the NEGATIVE form?

options.text: If buildings didn't flex, they wouldn't survive many major earthquakes.
If buildings don't flex, they wouldn't survive many major earthquakes.
If buildings didn't flex, they won't survive many major earthquakes.
If buildings didn't flex, they don't survive many major earthquakes.

questions.explanation_ar: النفي في الشرط الثاني يكون بـ didn't + مصدر في جملة الشرط، و wouldn't + مصدر في الجواب. الخيار a يطبّق ذلك بشكل صحيح.

questions.question_text: Choose the sentence where the second conditional uses formal inversion correctly.

options.text: If I were a seismologist, I would work to improve earthquake prediction.
Were I a seismologist, I would work to improve earthquake prediction.
Were I be a seismologist, I would work to improve earthquake prediction.
I were a seismologist, I would work to improve earthquake prediction.

questions.explanation_ar: في الإنجليزية الرسمية يمكن حذف if وتقديم were في بداية الجملة (Were I + subject complement) كأسلوب رسمي مكافئ للشرط الثاني.

questions.question_text: Which sentence uses the THIRD conditional (not second)?

options.text: If buildings were more flexible, they would survive earthquakes more easily.
If the warning system were better, fewer people would be in danger.
If the engineers had built it differently, the bridge would not have collapsed.
If seismologists had more funding, they could make faster progress.

questions.explanation_ar: الشرط الثالث يستخدم had + تصريف ثالث في جملة الشرط و would have + تصريف ثالث في الجواب للتعبير عن ندم على أحداث ماضية. الخيار c هو الشرط الثالث.

questions.question_text: The sentence 'If the Richter scale were not invented, scientists could not easily compare the strength of different earthquakes' uses the second conditional correctly.
questions.explanation_ar: صحيح. were not invented في جملة الشرط و could not easily compare في الجواب يُشكّلان الشرط الثاني الصحيح للتعبير عن موقف افتراضي مستحيل.

questions.question_text: Match each second conditional sentence to the type of situation it describes.

left_items: If I were a structural engineer, I would design flexible buildings.
If all countries adopted strict building codes, earthquake deaths would fall.
Were the ground more stable here, I would invest in this location.
If scientists could predict earthquakes, they might save millions of lives.
right_items: a hypothetical current situation using formal inversion
a personal imaginary role or occupation
a global policy change and its potential impact
a scientific capability that does not currently exist

correct_answer.left: If I were a structural engineer, I would design flexible buildings.
correct_answer.right: a personal imaginary role or occupation

correct_answer.left: If all countries adopted strict building codes, earthquake deaths would fall.
correct_answer.right: a global policy change and its potential impact

correct_answer.left: Were the ground more stable here, I would invest in this location.
correct_answer.right: a hypothetical current situation using formal inversion

correct_answer.left: If scientists could predict earthquakes, they might save millions of lives.
correct_answer.right: a scientific capability that does not currently exist

questions.explanation_ar: يُعبّر الشرط الثاني عن حالات مختلفة: دور مهني خيالي، تأثير سياسة عالمية، انعكاس الواقع، أو قدرة علمية غير موجودة حالياً.

questions.question_text: According to Passage B, what happened to buildings with advanced damping systems during the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake?

options.text: They were completely destroyed along with all conventional buildings
They suffered the same damage as buildings without damping systems
They remained operational while conventional structures suffered damage
They were evacuated as a precaution but remained structurally intact

questions.explanation_ar: أثبت زلزال تōهوكو 2011 فعالية أنظمة التخميد المتقدمة، إذ ظلّت المباني المجهّزة بها تعمل بشكل طبيعي بينما تعرّضت المباني التقليدية لأضرار جسيمة.

questions.question_text: According to Passage B, what is special about shape-memory alloys used in building construction?

options.text: They are lighter than conventional steel and reduce the total building weight
They become permanently stronger each time they experience an earthquake
They return to their original form after deformation and repair minor damage
They absorb heat from earthquakes and convert it into electrical energy

questions.explanation_ar: أوضح النص أن سبائك ذاكرة الشكل تتمتع بخاصية العودة إلى شكلها الأصلي بعد التشوّه، مما يُمكّنها من إصلاح الأضرار الطفيفة تلقائياً.

questions.question_text: Passage B states that 'buildings which flex and move fare better than their inflexible counterparts.' What does this suggest?

options.text: Rigid buildings are always safer and more durable than flexible ones
The best earthquake protection requires making buildings completely immovable
Allowing some movement during earthquakes is more effective than resisting all movement
Engineers should design buildings that collapse quickly to absorb earthquake energy

questions.explanation_ar: تُضمّن هذه الجملة مفارقة مثيرة: المباني القادرة على الحركة والانثناء أثناء الزلزال تُحقق أداءً أفضل من تلك المصمّمة لمقاومة أي حركة.

questions.question_text: According to Passage A, on the Richter scale each number represents a tenfold increase in wave amplitude.
questions.explanation_ar: صحيح. وضّح النص أن كل رقم على مقياس ريختر يمثّل زيادة بمقدار عشرة أضعاف في سعة الموجات، أي أن الزلزال 6.0 أقوى بعشر مرات من الزلزال 5.0.

questions.question_text: According to Passage A, the most devastating earthquake in recorded history occurred in China in the year _____.
questions.explanation_ar: ذكر النص أن أشد الزلازل تدميراً في التاريخ المسجَّل وقع في الصين عام 1556 وأودى بحياة ما يقارب 830,000 شخص.

questions.question_text: Match each Richter scale magnitude range from Passage A to its likely effect on buildings and people.

right_items: barely felt by humans; detected only by instruments
produces waves ten times larger than the one below
can rupture the ground surface and topple buildings
noticeable shaking; possible minor building damage

correct_answer.right: barely felt by humans; detected only by instruments
noticeable shaking; possible minor building damage
can rupture the ground surface and topple buildings
produces waves ten times larger than the one below

questions.explanation_ar: أوضح النص المقياس التدريجي لريختر: الزلازل الصغيرة لا تُحسّ، المتوسطة تُسبب اهتزازاً ملحوظاً، والكبيرة تشقّ الأرض وتُسقط المباني، وكل درجة أقوى بعشر مرات من السابقة.
